problem_13.natural_language: If `f` and `g` commute, then `ker(f)` and `range(f)` are invariant under `g`.
problem_13.lean:
theorem ker_range_invtSubmodule_of_commute
    {K V : Type*} [CommSemiring K] [AddCommMonoid V] [Module K V]
    (f g : Module.End K V)
    (hfg : f * g = g * f) :
    LinearMap.ker f ∈ g.invtSubmodule ∧ LinearMap.range f ∈ g.invtSubmodule
problem_13.natural_language_proof:
Proof: assume `f g = g f`. For the kernel, let `x ∈ ker f`, so `f x = 0`. Then
`f (g x) = (f g) x = (g f) x = g (f x) = 0`, hence `g x ∈ ker f`.
For the range, take `y ∈ range f`, so `y = f x` for some `x`. Then
`g y = g (f x) = (g f) x = (f g) x = f (g x)`, which lies again in `range f`.
problem_13.lean_proof:
by
  constructor
  · -- Prove LinearMap.ker f ∈ g.invtSubmodule
    have h₁ : LinearMap.ker f ≤ Submodule.comap g (LinearMap.ker f) := by
      intro x hx
      have h₂ : f (g x) = 0 := by
        calc
          f (g x) = (f * g) x := by
            rw [Module.End.mul_apply]
          _ = (g * f) x := by
            rw [hfg]
          _ = g (f x) := by
            rw [Module.End.mul_apply]
          _ = g 0 := by
            rw [hx]
          _ = 0 := by simp
      exact h₂
    exact h₁
  · -- Prove LinearMap.range f ∈ g.invtSubmodule
    have h₂ : LinearMap.range f ≤ Submodule.comap g (LinearMap.range f) := by
      intro y hy
      rcases hy with ⟨x, rfl⟩
      use g x
      have h₃ : (f * g) x = f (g x) := by
        rw [Module.End.mul_apply]
      have h₄ : (g * f) x = g (f x) := by
        rw [Module.End.mul_apply]
      have h₅ : (f * g) x = (g * f) x := by
        rw [hfg]
      calc
        f (g x) = (f * g) x := by rw [h₃]
        _ = (g * f) x := by rw [h₅]
        _ = g (f x) := by rw [h₄]
    exact h₂
